titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Victoria Mary Tyrrell, 19 November 1919
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to his sister Victoria Mary ("Minnie") Tyrell. Willie tells his sister that his regiment is about to move from France to Germany ("hunland") and describes the effects which the First World War has had on the French countryside. It seems that Willie was part of the British troops who occupied the Rhineland after Germany's defeat. This occupation began in December 1918 and lasted till June 1930. The British headquarters were situated in the German city of Cologne.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Tuesday 19/11/19 My dear Min Today I spent with Garrett in + near Le
Quesnoy. I told you in yesterdays letters
that I had a note from him telling me where he was
so I started at 9 this morning + found him
quite easily. So we had great chat. I met him
on the road and there we stood talkin of days
that are gone. But not for long. We went for
a walk to inspect the city + then returned to
Garretts mess for lunch. I started for home
about 3 pm as I wanted to get back before
dark. You see I am afraid of the dark
always !! G. is looking very well and he too is
en route to hunland but does not expect to
move for sometime yet. He is coming along to see
me one day soon. You might tell Sissie all
this. There was no post in today and so
no letters from anyone - but that means
a double ration tomorrow.
The weather has changed again and today
was cold + damp but no frost at all.
The roads are in a fearful state especially
in Mormal Forest. Nearly the whole forest
has been cut away + only the stumps of the
trees remain. The hun must have taken
thousands + thousands of tons of lumbers out
of it. Our moves I believe are to be Namur Liege - Cologne - + very nice too ! Not a word of news Very best love to all
Your most affec brother Willie P.S. My pilot Rowney is a great pal of Hamilton's
the dear little elve. They spent six weeks
together in next beds to each other in hospital
at Malta.
